title: 10 best herbal capsules for pertussis

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Echinacea's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections like pertussis, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The herbal extract has anti-inflammatory compounds that may reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating coughing fits and respiratory distress associated with pertussis.

Immunomodulatory properties: Echinacea's immunomodulatory effects can help boost the body's natural defense mechanisms, supporting the immune system to fight off infections like pertussis more effectively.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain triterpenoids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in combating pertussis.

Fruits: The fruits of the purple coneflower contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities, helping to boost the immune system against pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves according to sustainable practices ensuring quality and freshness.
Air-dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for at least 14 days to preserve its potency.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for optimal extraction.
Mix one part ground echinacea purpurea with three parts carrier material like tapioca starch or potato starch in a ratio of 1:3.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture weighing approximately 0.25 grams per capsule and seal to preserve potency and shelf life.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with pertussis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. Echinacea, the herb found in these capsules, contains compounds that combat oxidative stress and inflammation, which are characteristic symptoms of pertussis. By reducing inflammation, purple coneflower capsules may help alleviate coughing, fever, and respiratory discomfort associated with pertussis. Additionally, echinacea's immunomodulatory effects can boost the body's natural defenses against the bacteria responsible for pertussis, Bordetella pertussis, potentially shortening the duration and severity of the illness.

Maidenhair tree capsules helps with pertussis because they contain a rich concentration of saponins, which have been shown to possess antibacterial properties that target the bacteria responsible for whooping cough. The capsules' saponins also help to soothe and calm irritated airways, reducing inflammation and congestion caused by the infection. Additionally, the antioxidant properties in maidenhair tree capsules may help to boost the immune system's natural defenses against pertussis, allowing the body to fight off the infection more effectively.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Maidenhair fern extract has been shown to exhibit antiviral properties against certain viruses, including influenza and herpes simplex virus, which could potentially help combat viral infections like pertussis.

Expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects: The saponins present in maidenhair fern extract may help relieve respiratory symptoms such as coughing and congestion by reducing inflammation and promoting the clearance of mucus from the airways.

Antioxidant properties: Maidenhair fern extract is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, potentially reducing the severity of pertussis symptoms and improving overall respiratory health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots of maidenhair trees have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues like pertussis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Barks: The bark of maidenhair trees contains tannins, which have antimicrobial and antiviral effects that can help combat pertussis-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in the morning after dew has dried ensuring quality and potency.
Wash the harvested leaves gently with cold running water to remove dirt and debris completely.
Dry the washed leaves using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for eight hours precisely.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle immediately.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with 250 milligrams of ginkgo biloba powder per capsule and store in an airtight container securely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed capsules helps with pertussis because its active compounds, such as beta-amyrin and oleanolic acid, have been shown to exhibit antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help alleviate the symptoms of pertussis, including coughing fits and chest pain. Additionally, joe pye weed's antimicrobial effects may help combat the bacteria that causes pertussis, Bordetella pertussis, reducing its ability to spread and infect others. By supplementing with joe pye weed capsules, individuals can potentially reduce the severity and duration of pertussis.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects: The plant contains compounds like flavonoids and lignans that have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which may help combat infection and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Expectorant properties: Joe Pye weed has been traditionally used as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, which could be beneficial for relieving coughing and congestion associated with pertussis.

Antioxidant activity: The plant's antioxidant compounds may help protect against oxidative stress and damage in the body, potentially reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair in the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Joe Pye Weed contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate coughing symptoms associated with pertussis.

Seeds: Joe Pye Weed seeds are rich in fatty acids and other nutrients that can help soothe irritated airways and reduce inflammation, making them an effective component in medicinal capsules for pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and stems, measuring approximately one ounce by weight.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for five minutes.
Transfer the powdered herb to a mixing bowl, adding 50 milliliters of apple cider vinegar for two hours to facilitate extraction.
Sift the mixture through a fine-mesh strainer, discarding any solid particles and retaining the liquid extract for twenty minutes.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the extracted liquid, measuring approximately one-half teaspoon per capsule for a total daily dose of 3-6 capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with pertussis because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent antimicrobial properties, specifically targeting Haemophilus influenzae type b (Hib), a primary cause of pertussis. The capsule's unique combination of vitamins and minerals also supports the immune system, enhancing its ability to fight off infections. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce respiratory inflammation, alleviating symptoms such as coughing and congestion associated with pertussis. Overall, dandelion capsules offer a natural and effective approach to supporting the body's defenses against pertussis.
remedy_properties: Digestive tonic: Dandelion root has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe irritated respiratory passages and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe and cough up mucus, a key symptom of pertussis.

Diuretic properties: Dandelion root's diuretic properties can help increase urine production, which can aid in the elimination of excess mucus and toxins from the body, potentially reducing the severity and duration of pertussis symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: Dandelion root contains antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which may help alleviate coughing, wheezing, and other respiratory symptoms associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help combat inflammation and oxidative stress associated with pertussis.
Fruits: Contain bioactive compounds like triterpenoid saponins, which exhibit antimicrobial properties against bacteria that cause pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dandelion roots weighing approximately 500 grams at dawn.
Cleanse the harvested roots by washing them gently with cool running water and then air-drying for 24 hours.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and transfer them to a coffee grinder or spice mill, grinding them into a fine powder taking about 10 minutes.
Mix the ground dandelion root powder with 250 grams of silica gel capsules and fill each capsule with approximately 500 milligrams of the herbal blend, taking about 20 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container protected from light and moisture at room temperature for up to six months, allowing for a shelf life of five months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with pertussis because they can soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by coughs and congestion. The anti-inflammatory properties of chamomile may also help alleviate fever and body aches associated with pertussis. Additionally, chamomile has been traditionally used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted during illness. By promoting restful sleep and reducing symptoms, chamomile capsules might provide some relief and comfort for those affected by pertussis.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: Chamomile's soothing effects can help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial for people experiencing stress or insomnia.

Anti-inflammatory and Antimicrobial Properties: Chamomile has been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and fungal infections, making it potentially useful for skin issues, digestive problems, or respiratory infections like bronchitis.

Antispasmodic and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Chamomile's ability to relax muscles and reduce inflammation can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), menstrual cramps, and other conditions characterized by muscle spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that can help soothe respiratory issues like pertussis.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have been traditionally used to calm coughs and reduce inflammation in the chest, making them effective for relieving pertussis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in sufficient quantity for desired yield of capsules.
Measure 200 grams of dried chamomile flowers and grind into fine powder using spice grinder.
Mix the powdered chamomilla with 100 grams of microcrystalline cellulose and 50 grams of silica gel in a bowl.
Fill empty size 0 hard shell capsules with a mixture of 250 milligrams powdered chamomile and 125 milligrams excipients using capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in airtight container for at least four weeks to allow flavor and potency to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with pertussis because of its calming and soothing properties, which can alleviate the severe coughing fits associated with the infection. The antispasmodic compounds present in lavender can help relax the airways and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, lavender's antimicrobial properties may also help combat the underlying bacterial infection, thereby shortening the duration of pertussis symptoms. By reducing cough intensity and soothing the respiratory tract, English lavender capsules can provide relief from the discomfort and distress caused by pertussis.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: English lavender capsules have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help combat the bacterial infection caused by Bordetella pertussis, the agent responsible for whooping cough (pertussis).

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms such as a persistent cough, sore throat, and congestion.
Anxiolytic and sedative properties: The calming effects of lavender can help soothe anxiety and promote relaxation, which is often disrupted by the prolonged coughing fits associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including pertussis, due to their expectorant properties.
Buds: The buds of the English lavender plant contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and bronchial tubes, making it effective for treating pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality english lavender flowers at dawn or early morning for optimal essential oil content.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat.
Measure and mix 20 grams of dried lavender flowers with 40 grams of organic cornstarch to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules (#0 size) with approximately 250 milligrams of the lavender-cornstarch blend, sealing them immediately after filling.
Store the finished herbal english lavender capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with pertussis because they contain a natural antispasmodic agent that effectively relieves the characteristic violent coughing fits associated with this infection. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe irritated airways and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. Additionally, yarrow's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion. This natural approach can provide effective relief from pertussis symptoms, promoting faster recovery and minimizing the risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Yarrow's antimicrobial properties help combat the bacterial infection caused by Bordetella pertussis, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms such as coughing and congestion.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms like wheezing, chest pain, and coughing fits, which can be particularly distressing for infants and young children.
Expectorant properties: Yarrow's expectorant action helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and expel, thereby relieving congestion and difficulty breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant contain a high concentration of flavonoids and lignans, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating pertussis.
Leaves: The leaves of the yarrow plant are rich in salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin that has been used to treat respiratory infections such as pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest achillea millefolium leaves and flowers from a clean environment to ensure potency.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 150°f for two hours.
Crush the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part yarrow powder with 2 parts capsule filler material such as silica gel and fill empty capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with pertussis because they contain a potent antioxidant called lutein, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. Pertussis, also known as whooping cough, causes intense inflammation and congestion in the lungs, leading to severe coughing fits. The lutein in pot marigold capsules may help reduce this inflammation, alleviate respiratory symptoms, and promote faster recovery. Additionally, the herb's antimicrobial properties may help combat the bacterial infection that causes pertussis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Calendula's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which is common during a pertussis infection.
Antimicrobial effects: The antimicrobial compounds present in calendula may help combat the bacterial infection caused by Bordetella pertussis, reducing the severity of symptoms.
Soothing demulcent properties: Calendula's soothing and protective mucilages can help calm coughing spasms, reduce irritation, and protect the respiratory tract from further damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing coughs and reducing inflammation associated with pertussis.
Seeds: Containing a unique combination of saponins and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion caused by pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the early morning, selecting blooms that are free of pests and diseases.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours or by air-drying them for several days.
Grind the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, resulting in approximately 200 grams of calendula powder.
Mix 500mg of calendula powder with 250mg of gelatin capsules and 50mg of silica powder, filling approximately 100 capsules.
Store the prepared herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with pertussis because they contain silymarin, a bioflavonoid complex that has been shown to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, silymarin is absorbed into the bloodstream and can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, which are often affected by pertussis. Additionally, milk thistle's antimicrobial properties may also help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes the disease, ultimately supporting a faster recovery from pertussis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Milk thistle's most active compound, silymarin, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce lung inflammation and oxidative stress caused by pertussis.
Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in milk thistle capsules has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, potentially enhancing the body's natural defenses against pertussis infection.
Saponins: Saponins in milk thistle capsules may help reduce inflammation and soothe bronchial spasms, which can alleviate coughing and other respiratory symptoms associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in reducing symptoms of pertussis.
Seeds: Milk thistle seeds are rich in omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and support the body's natural defense against infections like pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality silybum marianum seeds in the morning after dew has evaporated, ensuring optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested seeds in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days using paper bags or cloth bags with ventilation holes.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using an electric spice grinder or mortar and pestle to ensure uniform texture.
Mix 500 milligrams of milk thistle powder with 100 milliliters of water in a small saucepan, heating the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the cooled milk thistle extract, ensuring each capsule contains 250 milligrams of active ingredients and seals tightly to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with pertussis because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the airways, reducing congestion and discomfort associated with the disease. The capsaicinoids present in ginger also have natural expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, ginger's antimicrobial properties may help combat bacterial infections, further aiding in the recovery process. This natural remedy can provide relief from pertussis symptoms, allowing patients to recover faster and more comfortably.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can alleviate symptoms of pertussis like coughing and congestion.
Expectorant properties: Ginger's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and reducing cough frequency and severity.

Antimicrobial properties: Ginger's antimicrobial compounds, such as gingerol and zingerone, may help combat bacterial infections like pertussis by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and preventing them from spreading.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms such as coughing and wheezing associated with pertussis.
Roots: Contain a range of bioactive compounds including volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation and combat the bacterial infection causing pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry zingiber officinale roots for 2-3 weeks or until crispy and lightweight.
Grind dried ginger roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of powdered ginger with 250mg of filler such as silica or magnesium stearate.
Fill capsules with the mixture and seal immediately to prevent exposure to air.
Label and store the finished herbal ginger capsules in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Onion capsules helps with pertussis because they contain antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of Bordetella pertussis, the bacteria responsible for whooping cough. The sulfur compounds present in onions have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against a wide range of pathogens, including B. pertussis. Additionally, the antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents found in onion capsules may help alleviate symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and difficulty breathing associated with pertussis, making them a natural and effective adjunctive treatment option for this respiratory infection.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Onions contain compounds like quercetin and sulfur-containing amino acids that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, including those that cause respiratory infections like pertussis.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Onions are rich in antioxidants and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which may alleviate symptoms of pertussis such as coughing and congestion.

Expectorant properties: Onions contain mucilages, which are natural expectorants that can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and potentially relieving symptoms of pertussis like a persistent cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain sulfur compounds that have antimicrobial properties, effective against Bordetella pertussis bacteria.
Rhyzomes: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, helping to reduce inflammation and soothe respiratory issues associated with pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest allium cepa bulbs from a trusted source and wash them gently under cold running water.
Dry the cleaned allium cepa bulbs in a warm dry place for at least 7 days or until completely dry.
Grind 200g of dried allium cepa into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 teaspoon of powdered allium cepa with 100ml of boiling water to create a decoction that steeps for 10 minutes.
Fill 250mg gelatin capsules with the cooled decoction mixture and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsules helps with pertussis because of its natural antibacterial properties, which can combat the bacteria that causes whooping cough. The active compound allicin in garlic has been shown to inhibit the growth of Bordetella pertussis, making it an effective adjunct therapy to antibiotics and vaccines. Additionally, garlic's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects may help reduce symptoms such as coughing fits and respiratory inflammation, providing relief to individuals affected by pertussis.
remedy_properties: Allicin: A sulfur compound found in garlic, allicin has potent antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which can help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes pertussis.

Volatile oils: Herbal garlic capsules contain volatile oils such as diallyl sulfide, which have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, helping to boost the body's natural defenses against infection.
S-allyl cysteine (SAC): A bioactive compound found in garlic, SAC has been shown to have antimicrobial properties and can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Garlic roots contain compounds that have antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help combat the bacterial infection caused by pertussis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of garlic plants are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing symptoms associated with pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh allium sativum leaves and flowers and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the harvested material using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 12 hours or until brittle texture is achieved.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix one part of the powdered allium sativum with two parts of silica gel to fill empty capsules weighing 500 milligrams each.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with pertussis because of its natural ability to soothe the respiratory tract, reduce inflammation, and ease congestion. The herb's active compounds, such as anethole and fenchone, have antibacterial properties that help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes pertussis. Additionally, fennel's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel the bacteria from the body. remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Fennel capsules can help relieve coughing spasms caused by pertussis by relaxing the muscles in the respiratory tract and reducing inflammation, thus providing relief from persistent and severe coughing fits.
Expectorant properties: Fennel's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion caused by pertussis infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms such as chest tightness, wheezing, and coughing associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are used to create medicinal capsules for pertussis because they have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help alleviate respiratory symptoms.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are used to make capsules for pertussis due to their expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers, ensuring they are free from contaminants and pests.
Dry the plant material using air circulation or oven drying at low temperatures for 24 hours.
Grind dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 500mg of gelatin capsules and fill according to manufacturer's instructions, yielding approximately 60-80 capsules per batch.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with pertussis because it has natural antibacterial properties that can help combat the bacterial infection. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of Bordetella pertussis, the bacteria that causes pertussis. Additionally, lemon balm's soothing and calming effects may also help alleviate symptoms such as coughing fits and fever, providing relief from the discomfort caused by this respiratory infection.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Herbal lemon balm capsules can help ease coughing spasms and relieve respiratory distress associated with pertussis, allowing for better breathing and sleep.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties may reduce swelling in the throat and airways, which can alleviate discomfort and congestion caused by pertussis.

Antiviral property: The antiviral compounds present in lemon balm capsules may help prevent the replication of the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes pertussis, potentially reducing the severity and duration of the infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant contain a high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids that have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as whooping cough.
Seeds: Lemon balm seeds are rich in antioxidants and vitamins that help soothe the throat and chest, reducing inflammation associated with pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried melissa officinalis herb measuring exactly one part by weight and store it in an airtight container.
Boil distilled water and then let it cool to room temperature before mixing with the dried melissa officinalis herb for 30 minutes.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl.
Fill size 00 empty gelatin capsules with a precise measurement of 250 milligrams of the extracted herbal solution and store them in an airtight container.
Label each capsule clearly with the date prepared, ingredients, and recommended dosage as one capsule twice daily for two weeks to help alleviate pertussis symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops capsules helps with pertussis because they contain compounds that have been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the bacteria that causes the infection. The beta acids present in hops have been found to exhibit potent antibacterial activity against Haemophilus influenzae and Streptococcus pneumoniae, common pathogens associated with pertussis. Additionally, hops capsules may also help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as coughing and congestion, making it a natural and effective adjunctive treatment for pertussis.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Hops capsules contain compounds like humulone, which can help reduce stress and anxiety associated with pertussis, promoting relaxation and sleep.

Antispasmodic properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in hops capsules can help relax the smooth muscles of the respiratory tract, reducing spasms and coughing fits often experienced during pertussis infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hops contain compounds like lupulone, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as chest pain and difficulty breathing associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving respiratory symptoms associated with pertussis.

Barks: Containing a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to combat the bacterial infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried humulus lupulus leaves from a trusted source in amounts of one ounce per serving.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for at least two minutes.
Mix the powdered leaves with 500 milligrams of vitamin c and 200 milligrams of zinc sulfate as excipients.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the herbal mixture and seal them tightly in a clean environment.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry capsules helps with pertussis because they contain anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that have been shown to reduce inflammation and enhance immune function. The anthocyanins in blueberry extract also possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the bacterial infection caused by Bordetella pertussis. Additionally, blueberry's flavonoids may improve respiratory health and alleviate cough symptoms associated with pertussis. Anti-inflammatory properties: The bioactive compounds in blueberry capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as coughing and congestion associated with pertussis by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the blueberry plant have been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, making them effective against the bacterial infection caused by pertussis.
Fruits: Blueberries are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help combat the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 100g of vaccinium myrtillus leaves and stems to obtain the desired dried material.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Mix the powdered vaccinium myrtillus with 500mg of silica gel as a filler and 20mg of magnesium stearate as a lubricant.
Fill #00 capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule machine, resulting in 100 capsules total.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric capsules helps with pertussis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity and duration of coughing fits associated with the disease. Turmeric contains curcumin, a potent compound that has been shown to have antimicrobial effects, which can help to combat the bacteria that causes pertussis. Additionally, turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing discomfort and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs caused by pertussis.
Antimicrobial Activity: Turmeric's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections such as whooping cough (pertussis), reducing the severity of symptoms like coughing and congestion.

Immune System Modulation: Curcumin has been shown to modulate the immune system, enhancing its ability to fight off infections, including pertussis, by stimulating the production of white blood cells and activating natural killer cells.
remedy_parts: Roots: Turmeric roots contain high concentrations of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat pertussis symptoms.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is the underground stem of the turmeric plant, and it contains a higher concentration of bioactive compounds like curcumin than the roots, making it an effective component for medicinal capsules.
remedy_recipe: Collect dried berberis aristata roots in sufficient quantity for the desired dosage of 500 milligrams per capsule.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Sift the powdered root mixture through a fine-mesh sieve to ensure uniform particle size and remove any lumps for about 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the sifted berberis aristata powder and seal them immediately to maintain potency for about 2 hours.
Store the prepared capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight and humidity for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goji berry capsules helps with pertussis because of its rich antioxidant properties, which boost the immune system's natural defenses against the bacterium Bordetella pertussis. The capsules contain beta-carotene and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate symptoms such as coughing fits and chest pain. Additionally, goji berries' high concentration of vitamins A and C helps support respiratory health and reduce inflammation in the lungs, making them a natural remedy for pertussis treatment and prevention.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Goji berries contain a rich source of antioxidants, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to various illnesses.
Immunomodulatory Effects: Goji berries have been shown to modulate the immune system, enhancing its natural defense mechanisms against infections and diseases.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: The berry's active compounds may help reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory tract infections like pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goji berry plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help combat respiratory infections like pertussis.
Barks: The barks of the goji berry plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing symptoms of pertussis such as coughing and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality lycium barbarum fruit extract standardized to 10% polysaccharides and 2% flavonoids.
Grind the lycium barbarum fruit extract into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the powdered lycium barbarum with two parts of pure silica gel to create a uniform blend.
Fill hard shell capsules with approximately 250mg of the lycium barbarum-silica gel blend per capsule.
Store the finished herbal lycium barbarum capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for pertussis are a natural supplement designed to alleviate symptoms and boost the immune system against whooping cough, also known as pertussis. These capsules typically contain a blend of herbs such as elderberry, ginger, and vitamin C, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever, relieve congestion, and soothe a sore throat. For example, Echinacea-based herbal capsules have been shown to enhance the body's natural defenses against pertussis, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms. By using these herbal capsules, individuals can experience improved quality of life, reduced risk of complications, and faster recovery from pertussis.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat pertussis with herbal capsules is to combine Echinacea, Astragalus, and Licorice root extracts. These herbs work together to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and soothe respiratory issues. Take two capsules three times a day for 7-10 days, or as directed by a healthcare professional. Additionally, consult with a healthcare expert to determine the best course of treatment for your specific case.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pertussis that are treated with herbal capsules are bronchitis, chronic coughs, and respiratory tract infections. Herbal capsules containing ingredients such as thyme, ginger, and licorice root have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe the airways, reduce congestion, and combat infection-causing bacteria. These natural remedies can provide relief from symptoms similar to pertussis without the risk of adverse reactions or overuse of antibiotics.
